name: LeaderObject
summary: Rhino Object that represents leader geometry and attributes

methods.name: ClearId
methods.summary: Resets the HasId property of the model component to false, if possible.

methods.name: ClearIndex
methods.summary: Resets the HasIndex property of the model component to false, if possible.

methods.name: ClearName
methods.summary: Resets the HasName property of the model component to false, if possible.

methods.name: CommitChanges
methods.summary: Moves changes made to this RhinoObject into the RhinoDoc.

returns.doc: true if changes were made.

methods.name: ConstructConstObject
methods.signature: protected void ConstructConstObject(Object parentObject, int subobjectIndex)
methods.summary: Assigns a parent object and a sub-object index to this.

params.name: parentObject
params.doc: The parent object.

params.name: subobjectIndex
params.doc: The sub-object index.

methods.name: CopyHistoryOnReplace
methods.summary: Gets the setting of the CopyOnReplace field in this object's history

returns.doc: true if this object has history and the field is set false otherwise

methods.name: CreateMeshes
methods.signature: public virtual int CreateMeshes(MeshType meshType, MeshingParameters parameters, bool ignoreCustomParameters)
methods.summary: Create meshes used to render and analyze surface and polysurface objects.

params.name: meshType
params.doc: type of meshes to create

params.name: parameters
params.doc: in parameters that control the quality of the meshes that are created

params.name: ignoreCustomParameters
params.doc: Default should be false. Should the object ignore any custom meshing parameters on the object's attributes

returns.doc: number of meshes created

methods.name: CustomRenderMeshesBoundingBox
methods.signature: public bool CustomRenderMeshesBoundingBox(MeshType mt, ViewportInfo vp, ref RenderMeshProvider.Flags flags, PlugIn plugin, DisplayPipelineAttributes attrs, out BoundingBox boundingBox)
methods.summary: Returns the bounding box of custom render primitives for this object .

params.name: mt
params.doc: The mesh type requested (render or analysis).

params.name: vp
params.doc: The viewport being rendered

params.name: flags
params.doc: See MeshProvider.Flags

params.name: plugin
params.doc: The requesting plug-in (typically the calling plugin)

params.name: attrs
params.doc: Display attributes for the caller - null if this is a full rendering.

params.name: boundingBox
params.doc: The requested bounding box

returns.doc: True if the process was a success

methods.name: DataCRC
methods.summary: Increments the Cyclic Redundancy Check value by this instance.

params.name: currentRemainder
params.doc: The current remainder value.

returns.doc: The updated remainder value.

methods.name: Description
methods.summary: Get a brief description of a object, including it's attributes and geometry.

params.name: textLog
params.doc: A text log for collecting the description.

methods.name: Dispose()
methods.summary: Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

methods.name: Dispose(Boolean)
methods.summary: For derived class implementers. This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method.You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true.Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

params.name: disposing
params.doc: true if the call comes from the Dispose() method; false if it comes from the Garbage Collector finalizer.

methods.name: DuplicateGeometry
methods.summary: Constructs a deep (full) copy of the geometry.

returns.doc: A copy of the internal geometry.

methods.name: EnableCustomGrips
methods.summary: Turns on/off the object's editing grips.

params.name: customGrips
params.doc: The custom object grips.

returns.doc: true if the call succeeded. If you attempt to add custom grips to an object that does not support custom grips, then false is returned.

methods.name: EnableVisualAnalysisMode
methods.summary: Used to turn analysis modes on and off.

params.name: mode
params.doc: A visual analysis mode.

params.name: enable
params.doc: true if the mode should be activated; false otherwise.

returns.doc: true if this object supports the analysis mode.

methods.name: EnsurePrivateCopy
methods.summary: If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

methods.name: Finalize
methods.summary: Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

methods.name: GetActiveVisualAnalysisModes
methods.summary: Gets a list of currently enabled analysis modes for this object.

returns.doc: An array of visual analysis modes. The array can be empty, but not null.

methods.name: GetBoundingBox
methods.summary: Called when Rhino wants the bounding box of this object. Viewpoert can be null. This is the default base class version that will go through to the C++ base class if it is called from the custom object

params.name: viewport
params.doc: For objects like labels or lights, the viewport in which the bounding box should be calculated. Otherwise null.

returns.doc: The bounding box.

methods.name: GetCustomRenderMeshParameter
methods.signature: public virtual IConvertible GetCustomRenderMeshParameter(Guid providerId, string parameterName)
methods.summary: Query the object for the value of a given named custom render mesh parameter.

params.name: providerId
params.doc: Id of the custom render mesh provider

params.name: parameterName
params.doc: Name of the parameter

returns.doc: IConvertible. Note that you can't directly cast from object, instead you have to use the Convert mechanism.

methods.name: GetDynamicTransform
methods.summary: While an object is being dynamically transformed (dragged, rotated, ...), the current transformation can be retrieved and used for creating dynamic display.

params.name: transform
params.doc: [Missing <param name="transform"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.DocObjects.RhinoObject.GetDynamicTransform(Rhino.Geometry.Transform@)"]

returns.doc: True if the object is being edited and its transformation is available. False if the object is not being edited, in which case the identity transform is returned.

methods.name: GetGrips
methods.summary: Returns grips for this object If grips are enabled. If grips are not enabled, returns null.

returns.doc: An array of grip objects; or null if there are no grips.

methods.name: GetGroupList
methods.summary: Allocates an array of group indices of length GroupCount. If GroupCount is 0, then this method returns null.

returns.doc: An array of group indices, or null if GroupCount is 0.

methods.name: GetHighlightedSubObjects
methods.summary: Gets a list of all highlighted sub-objects.

returns.doc: An array of all highlighted sub-objects; or null is there are none.

methods.name: GetMaterial(Boolean)
methods.summary: Gets material that this object uses based on it's attributes and the document that the object is associated with. In the rare case that a document is not associated with this object, null will be returned.

params.name: frontMaterial
params.doc: If true, gets the material used to render the object's front side

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.DocObjects.RhinoObject.GetMaterial(System.Boolean)"]

methods.name: GetMaterial(ComponentIndex)
methods.summary: Get the Material associated with the sub object identified by componentIndex if the component index is set to ComponentIndex.Unset then the top level material is returned.

params.name: componentIndex
params.doc: Returns the material associated with the specified sub object or the objects top level material if it is set to ComponentIndex.Unset

returns.doc: Returns the Material associated with the sub object identified by componentIndex if the component index is set to ComponentIndex.Unset then the top level material is returned.

methods.name: GetMaterial(ComponentIndex, Guid)
methods.signature: public Material GetMaterial(ComponentIndex componentIndex, Guid plugInId)
methods.summary: Get the Material associated with the sub object identified by componentIndex if the component index is set to ComponentIndex.Unset then the top level material is returned.

params.name: componentIndex
params.doc: Returns the material associated with the specified sub object or the objects top level material if it is set to ComponentIndex.Unset

params.name: plugInId
params.doc: The plug-in specific material to look for.

returns.doc: Returns the Material associated with the sub object identified by componentIndex if the component index is set to ComponentIndex.Unset then the top level material is returned.

methods.name: GetMaterial(ComponentIndex, Guid, ObjectAttributes)
methods.signature: public Material GetMaterial(ComponentIndex componentIndex, Guid plugInId, ObjectAttributes attributes)
methods.summary: Get the Material associated with the sub object identified by componentIndex if the component index is set to ComponentIndex.Unset then the top level material is returned.

params.name: componentIndex
params.doc: Returns the material associated with the specified sub object or the objects top level material if it is set to ComponentIndex.Unset

params.name: plugInId
params.doc: The plug-in specific material to look for.

params.name: attributes
params.doc: Optional object attributes used to determine the material source, if null the objects attributes are used.

returns.doc: Returns the Material associated with the sub object identified by componentIndex if the component index is set to ComponentIndex.Unset then the top level material is returned.

methods.name: GetMeshes
methods.summary: Get existing meshes used to render and analyze surface and polysurface objects.

params.name: meshType
params.doc: [Missing <param name="meshType"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.DocObjects.RhinoObject.GetMeshes(Rhino.Geometry.MeshType)"]

returns.doc: An array of meshes.

methods.name: GetObjectData
methods.signature: public virtual void GetObjectData(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)
methods.summary: Populates a System.Runtime.Serialization.SerializationInfo with the data needed to serialize the target object.

params.name: info
params.doc: The System.Runtime.Serialization.SerializationInfo to populate with data.

params.name: context
params.doc: The destination (see System.Runtime.Serialization.StreamingContext) for this serialization.

methods.name: GetRenderMaterial(Boolean)
methods.summary: Gets the RenderMaterial that this object uses based on it's attributes and the document that the object is associated with. If there is no RenderMaterial associated with this object then null is returned. If null is returned you should call GetMaterial to get the material used to render this object.

params.name: frontMaterial
params.doc: If true, gets the material used to render the object's front side otherwise; gets the material used to render the back side of the object.

returns.doc: If there is a RenderMaterial associated with this objects' associated Material then it is returned otherwise; null is returned.

methods.name: GetRenderMaterial(ComponentIndex)
methods.summary: Gets the RenderMaterial associated with this object if there is one. If there is no RenderMaterial associated with this object then null is returned. If null is returned you should call GetMaterial to get the material used to render this object.

params.name: componentIndex
params.doc: Returns the RenderMaterial associated with the specified sub object or the objects top level material if it is set to ComponentIndex.Unset

returns.doc: Returns the RenderMaterial associated with the sub object identified by componentIndex if the component index is set to then the top level RenderMaterail is returned. If this method returns null it means there is no RenderMaterial associated with the object or sub object so you should may GetMaterial get the objects generic material.

methods.name: GetRenderMaterial(ComponentIndex, Guid)
methods.signature: public RenderMaterial GetRenderMaterial(ComponentIndex componentIndex, Guid plugInId)
methods.summary: Gets the RenderMaterial associated with this object if there is one. If there is no RenderMaterial associated with this object then null is returned. If null is returned you should call GetMaterial to get the material used to render this object.

params.name: componentIndex
params.doc: Returns the RenderMaterial associated with the specified sub object or the objects top level material if it is set to

params.name: plugInId
params.doc: The plug-in specific material to look for.

returns.doc: Returns the RenderMaterial associated with the sub object identified by componentIndex if the component index is set to then the top level RenderMaterail is returned. If this method returns null it means there is no RenderMaterial associated with the object or sub object so you should may GetMaterial get the objects generic material.

methods.name: GetRenderMaterial(ComponentIndex, Guid, ObjectAttributes)
methods.signature: public RenderMaterial GetRenderMaterial(ComponentIndex componentIndex, Guid plugInId, ObjectAttributes attributes)
methods.summary: Gets the RenderMaterial associated with this object if there is one. If there is no RenderMaterial associated with this object then null is returned. If null is returned you should call GetMaterial to get the material used to render this object.

params.name: componentIndex
params.doc: Returns the RenderMaterial associated with the specified sub object or the objects top level material if it is set to

params.name: plugInId
params.doc: The plug-in specific material to look for.

params.name: attributes
params.doc: Optional object attributes used to determine the material source, if null the objects attributes are used.

returns.doc: Returns the RenderMaterial associated with the sub object identified by componentIndex if the component index is set to then the top level RenderMaterail is returned. If this method returns null it means there is no RenderMaterial associated with the object or sub object so you should may GetMaterial get the objects generic material.

methods.name: GetRenderMeshParameters()
methods.summary: Returns the meshing parameters that this object uses for generating render meshes. If this object does not have per-object meshing parameters, then the document's meshing parameters are returned.

returns.doc: The render meshing parameters.

methods.name: GetRenderMeshParameters(Boolean)
methods.summary: Returns the meshing parameters that this object uses for generating render meshes.

params.name: returnDocumentParametersIfUnset
params.doc: If true, then return the per-object meshing parameters for this object. If this object does not have per-object meshing parameters, then the document's meshing parameters are returned. If false, then return the per-object meshing parameters for this object. If this object does not have per-object meshing parameters, then null is returned.

returns.doc: The render meshing parameters if successful, null otherwise.

methods.name: GetRenderPrimitiveList(ViewportInfo, DisplayPipelineAttributes)
methods.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("This version is obsolete because it returns the obsolete RenderPrimitiveList - ie, the old school custom render meshes. Use CustomRenderMeshes instead.")] public RenderPrimitiveList GetRenderPrimitiveList(ViewportInfo viewport, DisplayPipelineAttributes attrs)
methods.summary: Build custom render mesh(es) for this object.

params.name: viewport
params.doc: The viewport being rendered.

params.name: attrs
params.doc: Attributes for the view mode you are supplying meshes for. Will be null if this is a modal rendering.

returns.doc: Returns a RenderPrimitiveList if successful otherwise returns null.

methods.name: GetRenderPrimitiveList(ViewportInfo, Boolean)
methods.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("This version is obsolete because it returns the obsolete RenderPrimitiveList - ie, the old school custom render meshes. Use CustomRenderMeshes instead.")] public RenderPrimitiveList GetRenderPrimitiveList(ViewportInfo viewport, bool preview)
methods.summary: Build custom render mesh(es) for this object.

params.name: viewport
params.doc: The viewport being rendered.

params.name: preview
params.doc: Type of mesh to build, if preview is true then a smaller mesh may be generated in less time, false is meant when actually rendering.

returns.doc: Returns a RenderPrimitiveList if successful otherwise returns null.

methods.name: GetSelectedSubObjects
methods.summary: Get a list of all selected sub-objects.

returns.doc: An array of sub-object indices, or null if there are none.

methods.name: GetSubObjects
methods.summary: Explodes the object into sub-objects. It is up to the caller to add the returned objects to the document.

returns.doc: An array of Rhino objects, or null if this object cannot be exploded.

methods.name: GetTextureChannels

returns.doc: Returns an array of channel Id's or an empty list if there are not mappings.

methods.name: GetTightBoundingBox(BoundingBox, Boolean, Transform)
methods.signature: protected virtual bool GetTightBoundingBox(ref BoundingBox tightBox, bool growBox, Transform xform)
methods.summary: Get tight bounding box of this Rhino object. The Rhino object is not modified.

params.name: tightBox
params.doc: Beginning and resulting tight box

params.name: growBox
params.doc: If true and the input tight_bbox is valid, then returned tight_bbox is the union of the input tight_bbox and the tight bounding box of this Rhino object.

params.name: xform
params.doc: If useXform==true, the tight bounding box of the transformed Rhino object is calculated.

returns.doc: true if the returned tight_bbox is set to a valid bounding box.

methods.name: HasCustomRenderMeshes
methods.signature: public bool HasCustomRenderMeshes(MeshType mt, ViewportInfo vp, ref RenderMeshProvider.Flags flags, PlugIn plugin, DisplayPipelineAttributes attrs)
methods.summary: Returns true if the object will has a set of custom render primitives - ie, CustomRenderMeshes will return non-null.

params.name: vp

params.name: flags
params.doc: See MeshProvider.Flags

params.name: plugin
params.doc: The requesting plug-in (typically the calling plugin)

params.name: attrs
params.doc: Display attributes for the caller - null if this is a full rendering.

returns.doc: Returns true if the object will has a set of custom render primitives

methods.summary: Modifies the highlighting of the object.

params.name: enable
params.doc: true if highlighting should be enabled.

returns.doc: true if the object is now highlighted.

methods.signature: public bool HighlightSubObject(ComponentIndex componentIndex, bool highlight)
methods.summary: Highlights a sub-object.

params.doc: A sub-object component index.

params.name: highlight
params.doc: true if the sub-object should be highlighted.

returns.doc: true if the sub-object is now highlighted.

methods.name: HistoryChildren
methods.summary: Gets the ids of the child Rhino objects of this object.

returns.doc: An array of Rhino object ids if successful, an empty array if the object has no children or on error.

methods.name: HistoryParents
methods.summary: Gets the ids of the parent Rhino objects of this object.

returns.doc: An array of Rhino object ids if successful, an empty array if the object has no parents or on error.

methods.name: InVisualAnalysisMode()
methods.summary: Reports if any visual analysis mode is currently active for an object.

returns.doc: true if an analysis mode is active; otherwise false.

methods.name: InVisualAnalysisMode(VisualAnalysisMode)
methods.summary: Reports if a visual analysis mode is currently active for an object.

params.name: mode
params.doc: The mode to check for. Use null if you want to see if any mode is active.

returns.doc: true if the specified analysis mode is active; otherwise false.

methods.name: IsActiveInViewport
methods.summary: Determine if this object is active in a particular viewport.
methods.remarks: The default implementation tests for space and viewport id. This handles things like testing if a page space object is visible in a modeling view.

params.name: viewport
params.doc: [Missing <param name="viewport"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.DocObjects.RhinoObject.IsActiveInViewport(Rhino.Display.RhinoViewport)"]

returns.doc: True if the object is active in viewport

methods.name: IsHighlighted
methods.summary: Check highlight state.

params.name: checkSubObjects
params.doc: If true and the entire object is not highlighted, and some subset of the object is highlighted, like some edges of a surface, then 3 is returned. If false and the entire object is not highlighted, then zero is returned.

returns.doc: 0: object is not highlighted.1: entire object is highlighted.3: one or more proper sub-objects are highlighted.

methods.name: IsMeshable
methods.summary: Returns true if the object is capable of having a mesh of the specified type

params.name: meshType
params.doc: [Missing <param name="meshType"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.DocObjects.RhinoObject.IsMeshable(Rhino.Geometry.MeshType)"]

returns.doc: [Missing <returns> documentation for "M:Rhino.DocObjects.RhinoObject.IsMeshable(Rhino.Geometry.MeshType)"]

methods.name: IsSelectable()
methods.summary: Reports if an object can be selected.
methods.remarks: Objects that are locked, hidden, or on locked or hidden layers cannot be selected. If IsSelectableWithGripsOn() returns false, then an that object is not selectable if it has grips turned on.

returns.doc: true if object is capable of being selected.

methods.name: IsSelectable(Boolean, Boolean, Boolean, Boolean)
methods.signature: public bool IsSelectable(bool ignoreSelectionState, bool ignoreGripsState, bool ignoreLayerLocking, bool ignoreLayerVisibility)
methods.summary: Reports if an object can be selected.
methods.remarks: Objects that are locked, hidden, or on locked or hidden layers cannot be selected. If IsSelectableWithGripsOn() returns false, then an that object is not selectable if it has grips turned on.

params.name: ignoreSelectionState
params.doc: If true, then selected objects are selectable. If false, then selected objects are not selectable.

params.name: ignoreGripsState
params.doc: If true, then objects with grips on can be selected. If false, then the value returned by the object's IsSelectableWithGripsOn() function decides if the object can be selected.

params.name: ignoreLayerLocking
params.doc: If true, then objects on locked layers are selectable. If false, then objects on locked layers are not selectable.

params.name: ignoreLayerVisibility
params.doc: If true, then objects on hidden layers are selectable. If false, then objects on hidden layers are not selectable.

returns.doc: true if object is capable of being selected.

methods.name: IsSelected
methods.summary: Check selection state.

params.name: checkSubObjects
params.doc: (false is good default) If true and the entire object is not selected, and some subset of the object is selected, like some edges of a surface, then 3 is returned. If false and the entire object is not selected, then zero is returned.

returns.doc: 0 = object is not selected. 1 = object is selected. 2 = entire object is selected persistently. 3 = one or more proper sub-objects are selected.

methods.name: IsSubObjectHighlighted
methods.summary: Determines if a sub-object is highlighted.

params.name: componentIndex
params.doc: A sub-object component index.

returns.doc: true if the sub-object is highlighted.

methods.name: IsSubObjectSelectable
methods.signature: public bool IsSubObjectSelectable(ComponentIndex componentIndex, bool ignoreSelectionState)
methods.summary: Reports if a sub-object can be selected.
methods.remarks: Objects that are locked, hidden, or on locked or hidden layers cannot be selected. If IsSelectableWithGripsOn() returns false, then that object is not selectable if it has grips turned on.

params.name: componentIndex
params.doc: index of sub-object to check.

params.name: ignoreSelectionState
params.doc: If true, then selected objects are selectable. If false, then selected objects are not selectable.

returns.doc: true if the specified sub-object can be selected.

methods.name: IsSubObjectSelected
methods.summary: Check sub-object selection state.
methods.remarks: A sub-object cannot be persistently selected.

params.name: componentIndex
params.doc: Index of sub-object to check.

returns.doc: true if the sub-object is selected.

methods.name: IsValidWithLog
methods.summary: Determines if an object is valid. Also provides a report on errors if this object happens not to be valid.

params.name: log
params.doc: A textual log. This out parameter is assigned during this call.

returns.doc: true if this object is valid; false otherwise.

methods.name: LockId
methods.summary: Locks the component Id property.

methods.name: LockIndex
methods.summary: Locks the component Index property.

methods.name: LockName
methods.summary: Locks the component Name property.

methods.name: MemoryEstimate
methods.summary: Computes an estimate of the number of bytes that this object is using in memory. Note that this is a runtime memory estimate and does not directly compare to the amount of space take up by the object when saved to a file.

returns.doc: The estimated number of bytes this object occupies in memory.

methods.name: MeshCount
methods.signature: public virtual int MeshCount(MeshType meshType, MeshingParameters parameters)
methods.summary: RhinoObjects can have several different types of meshes and different numbers of meshes. A b-rep can have a render and an analysis mesh on each face. A mesh object has a single render mesh and no analysis mesh. Curve, point, and annotation objects have no meshes.

params.name: meshType
params.doc: type of mesh to count

params.name: parameters
params.doc: if not null and if the object can change its mesh (like a brep), then only meshes that were created with these mesh parameters are counted.

returns.doc: number of meshes

methods.name: RenderMeshes
methods.signature: public RenderMeshes RenderMeshes(MeshType mt, ViewportInfo vp, List<InstanceObject> ancestry, ref RenderMeshProvider.Flags flags, PlugIn plugin, DisplayPipelineAttributes attrs)
methods.summary: Returns a set of custom render primitives for this object.

params.name: mt
params.doc: The mesh type requested (render or analysis).

params.name: vp
params.doc: The viewport being rendered

params.name: ancestry
params.doc: The ancestry tree - only used for by-parent object properties assignments.

params.name: flags
params.doc: See MeshProvider.Flags

params.name: plugin
params.doc: The requesting plug-in (typically the calling plugin)

params.name: attrs
params.doc: Display attributes for the caller - null if this is a full rendering.

returns.doc: Returns a set of custom render primitives for this object

methods.name: Select(Boolean)
methods.summary: Selects an object.
methods.remarks: Objects that are locked, hidden, or on locked or hidden layers cannot be selected. If IsSelectableWithGripsOn() returns false, then an that object is not selectable if it has grips turned on.

params.name: on
params.doc: The new selection state; true activates selection.

returns.doc: 0: object is not selected.1: object is selected.2: object is selected persistently.

methods.name: Select(Boolean, Boolean)
methods.signature: public int Select(bool on, bool syncHighlight)
methods.summary: Selects an object.
methods.remarks: Objects that are locked, hidden, or on locked or hidden layers cannot be selected. If IsSelectableWithGripsOn() returns false, then an that object is not selectable if it has grips turned on.

params.name: on
params.doc: The new selection state; true activates selection.

params.name: syncHighlight
params.doc: If true, then the object is highlighted if it is selected and not highlighted if is not selected. Highlighting can be and stay out of sync, as its specification is independent.

returns.doc: 0: object is not selected.1: object is selected.2: object is selected persistently.

methods.name: Select(Boolean, Boolean, Boolean, Boolean, Boolean, Boolean)
methods.signature: public int Select(bool on, bool syncHighlight, bool persistentSelect, bool ignoreGripsState, bool ignoreLayerLocking, bool ignoreLayerVisibility)
methods.summary: Selects an object.
methods.remarks: Objects that are locked, hidden, or on locked or hidden layers cannot be selected. If IsSelectableWithGripsOn() returns false, then an that object is not selectable if it has grips turned on.

params.name: on
params.doc: The new selection state; true activates selection.

params.name: syncHighlight
params.doc: If true, then the object is highlighted if it is selected and unhighlighted if is not selected. Highlighting can be and stay out of sync, as its specification is independent.

params.name: persistentSelect
params.doc: Objects that are persistently selected stay selected when a command terminates.

params.name: ignoreGripsState
params.doc: If true, then objects with grips on can be selected. If false, then the value returned by the object's IsSelectableWithGripsOn() function decides if the object can be selected when it has grips turned on.

params.name: ignoreLayerLocking
params.doc: If true, then objects on locked layers can be selected. If false, then objects on locked layers cannot be selected.

params.name: ignoreLayerVisibility
params.doc: If true, then objects on hidden layers can be selectable. If false, then objects on hidden layers cannot be selected.

returns.doc: 0: object is not selected.1: object is selected.2: object is selected persistently.

methods.name: SelectSubObject(ComponentIndex, Boolean, Boolean)
methods.signature: public int SelectSubObject(ComponentIndex componentIndex, bool select, bool syncHighlight)
methods.summary: Reports if an object can be selected.
methods.remarks: Objects that are locked, hidden, or on locked or hidden layers cannot be selected. If IsSelectableWithGripsOn() returns false, then an that object is not selectable if it has grips turned on.

params.name: componentIndex
params.doc: Index of sub-object to check.

params.name: select
params.doc: The new selection state; true activates selection.

params.name: syncHighlight
params.doc: (default=true) If true, then the object is highlighted if it is selected and unhighlighted if is not selected.

returns.doc: 0: object is not selected 1: object is selected 2: object is selected persistently.

methods.name: SelectSubObject(ComponentIndex, Boolean, Boolean, Boolean)
methods.signature: public int SelectSubObject(ComponentIndex componentIndex, bool select, bool syncHighlight, bool persistentSelect)
methods.summary: Reports if an object can be selected.
methods.remarks: Objects that are locked, hidden, or on locked or hidden layers cannot be selected. If IsSelectableWithGripsOn() returns false, then an that object is not selectable if it has grips turned on.

params.name: componentIndex
params.doc: Index of sub-object to check.

params.name: select
params.doc: The new selection state; true activates selection.

params.name: syncHighlight
params.doc: (default=true) If true, then the object is highlighted if it is selected and unhighlighted if is not selected.

params.name: persistentSelect
params.doc: When true, selection persists even after the current command terminates.

returns.doc: 0: object is not selected1: object is selected2: object is selected persistently.

methods.name: SetCopyHistoryOnReplace
methods.summary: If this object has a history record, the CopyOnReplace field is set When an object is replaced in a document and the old object has a history record with this field set, the history record is copied and attached to the new object. That allows a descendant object to continue the history linkage after it is edited.

params.name: bCopy
params.doc: [Missing <param name="bCopy"/> documentation for "M:Rhino.DocObjects.RhinoObject.SetCopyHistoryOnReplace(System.Boolean)"]

returns.doc: true if successful, otherwise false.

methods.name: ShortDescription

returns.doc: Returns true if custom render mesh(es) will get built for this object.

params.doc: The viewport being rendered.

params.name: preview
params.doc: Type of mesh to build. If attributes is non-null then a smaller mesh may be generated in less time, false is meant when actually rendering.

returns.doc: Returns true if custom render mesh(es) will get built for this object.

params.name: frame

methods.name: TryGetRenderPrimitiveBoundingBox(ViewportInfo, DisplayPipelineAttributes, BoundingBox)
methods.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("Use CustomRenderMeshesBoundingBox instead")] public bool TryGetRenderPrimitiveBoundingBox(ViewportInfo viewport, DisplayPipelineAttributes attrs, out BoundingBox boundingBox)
methods.summary: Get the bounding box for the custom render meshes associated with this object.

params.name: viewport
params.doc: The viewport being rendered.

params.name: attrs
params.doc: Attributes for the view mode you are supplying meshes for. Will be null if this is a modal rendering.

params.name: boundingBox
params.doc: This will be set to BoundingBox.Unset on failure otherwise it will be the bounding box for the custom render meshes associated with this object.

returns.doc: Returns true if the bounding box was successfully calculated otherwise returns false on error.

methods.name: TryGetRenderPrimitiveBoundingBox(ViewportInfo, Boolean, BoundingBox)
methods.signature: [ObsoleteAttribute("Use CustomRenderMeshesBoundingBox instead")] public bool TryGetRenderPrimitiveBoundingBox(ViewportInfo viewport, bool preview, out BoundingBox boundingBox)
methods.summary: Get the bounding box for the custom render meshes associated with this object.

params.name: viewport
params.doc: The viewport being rendered.

params.name: preview
params.doc: Type of mesh to build, if preview is true then a smaller mesh may be generated in less time, false is meant when actually rendering.

params.name: boundingBox
params.doc: This will be set to BoundingBox.Unset on failure otherwise it will be the bounding box for the custom render meshes associated with this object.

returns.doc: Returns true if the bounding box was successfully calculated otherwise returns false on error.

methods.name: UnhighlightAllSubObjects
methods.summary: Removes highlighting from all sub-objects.

returns.doc: The number of changed sub-objects.

methods.name: UnselectAllSubObjects
methods.summary: Removes selection from all sub-objects.

returns.doc: The number of unselected sub-objects.

properties.name: AnnotationGeometry
properties.summary: Get the annotation base geometry for this object

properties.name: Attributes
properties.summary: Gets or sets the object attributes.

properties.name: ComponentStatus
properties.summary: Gets or sets the component status of the model component.

properties.name: ComponentType
properties.summary: Returns ModelGeometry.

properties.name: DeletedName
properties.summary: Gets the name of a component that is deleted.

properties.name: DisplayText
properties.summary: Gets the text that is displayed to users.

properties.name: Disposed
properties.summary: Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.

properties.name: Document
properties.summary: Gets the document that owns this object.

properties.name: Geometry
properties.summary: Gets the underlying geometry for this object. All rhino objects are composed of geometry and attributes.

properties.name: GripsOn
properties.summary: Gets or sets the activation state of object default editing grips.

properties.name: GripsSelected
properties.summary: true if grips are turned on and at least one is selected.

properties.name: GroupCount
properties.summary: Number of groups object belongs to.

properties.name: HasDynamicTransform
properties.summary: True if the object has a dynamic transformation

properties.name: HasId
properties.summary: Returns a value indicating whether the component has an ID.

properties.name: HasIndex
properties.summary: Returns a value indicating whether the component has an Index.

properties.name: HasMeasurableTextFields
properties.summary: Test if the text in the annotation object contains fields that involve length, area, or volume measurements

properties.name: HasName
properties.summary: Returns a value indicating whether the component has a Name.

properties.name: HasSubobjectMaterials
properties.summary: Will be true if the object contains sub object meshes with materials that are different than the top level object.

properties.name: HasUserData
properties.summary: Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.

properties.name: Id
properties.summary: Every object has a Guid (globally unique identifier, also known as UUID, or universally unique identifier). The default value is Guid.Empty. When an object is added to a model, the value is checked. If the value is Guid.Empty, a new Guid is created. If the value is not null but it is already used by another object in the model, a new Guid is created. If the value is not Guid.Empty and it is not used by another object in the model, then that value persists. When an object is updated, by a move for example, the value of ObjectId persists. This value is the same as the one returned by this.Attributes.ObjectId.

properties.name: IdIsLocked
properties.summary: Returns a value indicating whether the component ID is already locked.

properties.name: Index
properties.summary: Gets or sets the model component index attribute.

properties.name: IndexIsLocked
properties.summary: Returns a value indicating whether the component Index is already locked.

properties.name: InstanceDefinitionModelSerialNumber
properties.summary: When a component is in a model as part of the information required for a linked instance definition, this value identifies the linked instance definition reference model.
properties.remarks: Reference components are not saved in .3dm archives. Typically this value is set and locked by the code that adds a component to a model. This value is not saved in .3dm archives.

properties.name: IsComponentStatusLocked
properties.summary: The component status itself can be locked. This returns an indication.

properties.name: IsDeletable
properties.summary: Some objects cannot be deleted, like grips on lights and annotation objects.

properties.name: IsDeleted
properties.summary: true if the object is deleted. Deleted objects are kept by the document for undo purposes. Call RhinoDoc.UndeleteObject to undelete an object.

properties.name: IsDocumentControlled
properties.summary: If true this object may not be modified. Any properties or functions that attempt to modify this object when it is set to "IsReadOnly" will throw a NotSupportedException.

properties.name: IsHidden
properties.summary: An object must be in one of three modes: normal, locked or hidden. If an object is in normal mode, then the object's layer controls visibility and selectability. If an object is locked, then the object's layer controls visibility by the object cannot be selected. If the object is hidden, it is not visible and it cannot be selected.

properties.name: IsInstanceDefinitionGeometry
properties.summary: true if the object is used as part of an instance definition.

properties.name: IsLocked
properties.summary: An object must be in one of three modes: normal, locked or hidden. If an object is in normal mode, then the object's layer controls visibility and selectability. If an object is locked, then the object's layer controls visibility by the object cannot be selected. If the object is hidden, it is not visible and it cannot be selected.

properties.name: IsNormal
properties.summary: An object must be in one of three modes: normal, locked or hidden. If an object is in normal mode, then the object's layer controls visibility and selectability. If an object is locked, then the object's layer controls visibility by the object cannot be selected. If the object is hidden, it is not visible and it cannot be selected.

properties.name: IsPictureFrame
properties.summary: Returns true if the object is a picture frame. A picture frame object is an object that displays a texture map in all views.

properties.name: IsReference
properties.summary: Gets a value indicating if an object is a reference object. An object from a work session reference model is a reference object and cannot be modified. An object is a reference object if, and only if, it is on a reference layer.

properties.name: IsSolid
properties.summary: Returns true if object is a closed solid, otherwise false.

properties.name: IsSystemComponent
properties.summary: True if this model component is a system constant. An incomplete list of system constant model components is below:ON_ModelComponent::Unset ON_InstanceDefinition::Empty ON_Linetype::UnsetON_Linetype::ContinuousON_Linetype::ByLayerON_Linetype::ByParent ON_Layer::UnsetON_Layer::Default ON_TextStyle::UnsetON_TextStyle::DefaultON_TextStyle::ByLayerON_TextStyle::ByParent ON_DimStyle::UnsetON_DimStyle::DefaultON_DimStyle::DefaultInchDecimalON_DimStyle::DefaultInchFractionalON_DimStyle::DefaultFootInchArchitectureON_DimStyle::DefaultMillimeterSmallON_DimStyle::DefaultMillimeterLargeON_DimStyle::DefaultMillimeterArchitecture

properties.name: IsValid
properties.summary: Tests an object to see if it is valid.

properties.name: LeaderGeometry
properties.summary: Get the leader geometry for this object.

properties.name: ModelSerialNumber
properties.summary: A value identifying the model that manages this component.
properties.remarks: If the component is being managed by a model, this value identifies the model. In Rhino, this value is the document runtime serial number. Typically this value is set and locked by the code that adds a component to a model. This value is not saved in .3dm archives.

properties.name: Name
properties.summary: Rhino objects have optional text names. More than one object in a model can have the same name and some objects may have no name.

properties.name: NameIsLocked
properties.summary: Returns a value indicating whether the component Name is already locked.

properties.name: ObjectType
properties.summary: Gets the Rhino-based object type.

properties.name: ReferenceModelSerialNumber
properties.summary: When a component is in a model for reference, this value identifies the reference model.
properties.remarks: Reference components are not saved in .3dm archives. Typically this value is set and locked by the code that adds a component to a model. This value is not saved in .3dm archives.

properties.name: RenderMaterial
properties.summary: Gets the render material associated with this object or null if there is none. This does not pay attention to the material source and will not check parent objects or layers for a RenderMaterial.

properties.name: RuntimeSerialNumber
properties.summary: Gets the objects runtime serial number.

properties.name: SubobjectMaterialComponents
properties.summary: (Inherited from RhinoObject.)

properties.name: UserData
properties.summary: List of custom information that is attached to this class.

properties.name: UserDictionary
properties.summary: Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use shareable set of information.

properties.name: Visible
properties.summary: Gets the object visibility.
